children.label: A
children.text: $600,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2022 and 2023; and

children.label: B
children.text: $300,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2024 through 2026.

children.heading: Spending and borrowing authority
children.label: 2 Spending and borrowing authority
children.text: Spending and borrowing authority for a fiscal year to enter into Federal credit instruments shall be promptly apportioned to the Secretary on a fiscal-year basis.

children.heading: Reestimates
children.label: 3 Reestimates
children.text: If the subsidy amount of a Federal credit instrument is reestimated, the cost increase or decrease of the reestimate shall be borne by, or benefit, the general fund of the Treasury, consistent with .section 661c(f) of title 2

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: 4 Administrative costs
children.text: Of the amounts made available to carry out the CIFIA program, the Secretary may use not more than $9,000,000 (as indexed for United States dollar inflation from (as measured by the Consumer Price Index)) each fiscal year for the administration of the CIFIA program.November 15, 2021

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, execution of a term sheet by the Secretary of a Federal credit instrument that uses amounts made available under the CIFIA program shall impose on the United States a contractual obligation to fund the Federal credit investment.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 2 Availability
children.text: Amounts made available to carry out the CIFIA program for a fiscal year shall be available for obligation on October 1 of the fiscal year.
